perturbation.original_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with Fazync via email to IHSI's CEO, Devon Jones, and delivered through Bravatek or a Fazync -designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by Fazync at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at Fazync bank account.
perturbation.changed_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with Fazync, Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at Fazync bank account.
perturbation.explanation: The modification omits the crucial details of how the 'lead-finder fee' is determined (specifically, the email to IHSI's CEO Devon Jones and the range of 10%-20% of project revenue and the fact that the exact fee is finally decided by Fazync). This creates an in-text contradiction because while Section 4 still mentions a 'lead-finder fee,' the method of its calculation and the party responsible for its determination are now undefined, making enforcement ambiguous and potentially contradicting the original intent of a mutually agreed-upon fee structure. The text now creates a potential situation where Fazync can decide to ignore the agreement by setting the fee to 0%.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When custom Products are designed, developed and to be delivered to Bravatek-identified perspective clients, the parties shall agree to a proposed sales price for use during the project in writing prior to the commencement of each project. For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with Fazync, Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at Fazync bank account.
perturbation.justification: The omission of details regarding the 'lead-finder fee' calculation and determination process in the changed text directly contradicts the original intent outlined in both the original text and the contradicted text. The contradicted text explicitly states that "Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee," implying a defined and agreed-upon amount or method of calculation. By removing the specific details of the fee determination (email registration, 10-20% range, Fazync's decision), the changed text creates ambiguity and uncertainty, potentially allowing Fazync to arbitrarily set the fee to zero or an unacceptable amount, rendering the original agreement regarding the 'lead-finder fee' unenforceable and in contradiction with the expectation of a mutually understood compensation. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential information" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information, documents and materials related to client projects of party and products, services and business of each of the parties. Fazync and Bravatek agree to maintain in strict confidence and not to disclose or disseminate, or to use for any purposes other than performance of the projects, the Confidential Information disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: "Confidential information" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information, documents and materials related to client projects of party and products, services and business of each of the parties. Fazync and Bravatek agree to maintain in strict confidence and not to disclose or disseminate the Confidential Information disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'or to use for any purposes other than performance of the projects', the altered text creates an in-text contradiction within Section 5. Initially, the clause strictly limited the use of Confidential Information solely to project performance. Now, the modified version still requires maintaining confidentiality but removes the explicit allowance for utilizing the information for project-related activities. This omission now forbids them to use confidential information for the purpose of the project. This restricts how confidential information can be used, potentially hindering the core objective of the alliance, contradicting the agreement's purpose and creates an internal contradiction with Section 1-3 that details project activies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Confidential information" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information, documents and materials related to client projects of party and products, services and business of each of the parties. Fazync and Bravatek agree to maintain in strict confidence and not to disclose or disseminate the Confidential Information disclosed. The obligation of non-disclosure shall not apply to the following: a. Information at or after such time that is publicly available through no fault of either party b. Information at or after such time that is disclosed to either party by a third party entitled to disclose such information c. Information which is required by law to be disclosed to federal, state or local authorities.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed Fazync and Bravatek to use confidential information for project performance. The changed text removes this allowance, creating a contradiction with the agreement's implicit purpose of enabling project-related activities using confidential information. The 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition is directly applicable, as the removal of 'or to use for any purposes other than performance of the projects' creates a conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Such notice must be given ninety (90) days prior to expiration of the original term.
perturbation.changed_text: Such notice must be given prior to expiration of the original term.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'ninety (90) days' the changed text now lacks a definite timeframe for notifying the other party about non-renewal. This creates an in-text contradiction: while the agreement mentions a renewal process that requires notification, it fails to specify a minimum notice period, creating uncertainty about when such notification must be given, thus, contradicting its own renewal clause. Section 7 requires notice, but without a specified timeframe, creates ambiguity and making that specific condition not applicable. This creates a contradiction for the term section of the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given prior to expiration of the original term. This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated a 'ninety (90) days' notice period prior to the expiration of the original term for non-renewal. The changed text eliminates this specific timeframe, only requiring notice 'prior to expiration.' This omission creates a contradiction because the contradicted text relies on a specific notice period, while the changed text provides no such period, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes regarding the timing of the notice of non-renewal. The removal breaks the consistency of the contract term.
